Text Templates & Triggers

Click Edit to change wording. Use variable chips to insert dynamic fields. Changes save permanently.

MMS Image Uploads

T1, T6, and T8 support image attachments. Images save permanently.

📅 Calendar Event Body Templates

Each appointment type has its own editable body — used as both the Google Calendar event description and the invite email sent to attendees. Supports {{Customer Name}}, {{Job Number}}, {{Date}}, {{Time}}, {{Address}}, {{Salesperson}}, {{Insurance Company}}, {{Phone}}. Changes save permanently.

💬 Scheduled Appointment SMS Templates

One SMS template per appointment type — sent to the full group thread when an appointment is scheduled. Variables: {{First Name}} {{Appointment Type}} {{Date}} {{Time}} {{Address}}. Changes save permanently.

💬 Rescheduled Appointment SMS Templates

One SMS template per appointment type — sent to the full group thread when an appointment is rescheduled. Same variables available. Changes save permanently.

🔔 Day-Before Reminder SMS Templates

⚠️ These templates are managed in Google Sheets — edit them directly in the "ReminderTemplates" tab of your spreadsheet, not here. Changes in the sheet are picked up automatically by the reminder Worker on its next run. The previews below are read-only and reflect what was last synced from this device.

Brand Logo

RingCentral Inbound SMS Webhook

Register your Cloudflare Worker to log inbound SMS replies to AccuLynx. Do once.

Reminder Template Sync

HOW TO PREPARE
AND WHAT TO EXPECT

TC Roofing & Restorations is preparing to come out and perform a roofing job at your home or business. In order to ensure that the process goes as smoothly as possible for you, we have compiled a list of steps and precautions to take in preparing for this job. If after reading this list, you have any uncertainties or additional questions about the project, please bring them to our attention before the work begins.

1. When shingles are delivered to your home, please check the color. Notify TC Roofing & Restorations immediately if the color is not what you ordered.
2. Be prepared for the noise! There will be constant hammering for a day or two depending on the size of your roof.
3. Keep small children and pets away from the work area.
4. Remove all loose items from wall shelves and walls: pictures, plates, figurines, loose fixtures. Hammering will create unavoidable vibrations that can cause damage to these items. We will not be responsible for any damages inside due to vibrations.
5. Remove all items from around the house where roofing debris might fall: patio and pool furniture, potted plants, gardening equipment, and hanging flags. Our crew will be happy to help. Leaves or blooms may be knocked off the plants near your home during the job. We try to protect the plants as best as possible, but sometimes the layout of the landscape makes this unavoidable. During the holidays, TC is not responsible for removing decorations or holiday lights. Please remove any decorations prior to the build.
6. During the roof project, dust may fall from the rafters and undersides of the decking. Any items in the attic which may get damaged should be protected, we will be happy to help, please notify us.
7. We will need access to your driveway for materials, to load debris and clean up. Please park your cars, motorcycles, bicycles, boats, etc. away from your driveway and the work. We will not start the work if there are vehicles that may get damaged.
8. TC Roofing & Restorations orders the materials which may arrive at your home up to 5 days prior to the build. Normally materials are placed on the driveway of the home. We are happy to coordinate alternative arrangements in certain cases with prior notice from you, the homeowner. The materials are weather-proof and will not be damaged with the elements.
9. TC Roofing & Restorations will always order slightly more materials to avoid project delays due to shortages. The materials left over belong to TC Roofing & Restorations. Upon request, TC Roofing & Restorations will leave partial bundles for our clients. Adversely, TC Roofing & Restorations will not charge our homeowner for additional materials if needed to complete the project, unless there is rotted decking. If rotted decking is discovered, we will document the damage and replace the decking to meet code. The charge is $75 dollars per sheet. Wood rot to decking or siding is not covered by insurance because it qualifies as wear and tear and occurs over a period of time as opposed to a single weather event.
10. If the home's driveway has cracks, the homeowner shall notify TC Roofing & Restorations to make arrangements to avoid damaging the driveway further with heavy trailers, dumpsters and materials. However, use of the driveway may be inevitable and TC Roofing & Restorations is not responsible for further cracking of the driveway. TC Roofing & Restorations makes every effort to protect your home by use of pull behind trailers on wheels which greatly minimizes the chances this may occur.
11. Precautions are taken to prevent gutter and gutter guard damage. However, there are instances when damage to gutters is unavoidable, especially on a very steep roof. We will take all necessary precautions, but are not responsible for damaged gutters.
12. For each day it rains, work on your roof could be delayed. TC Roofing & Restorations' roofers are instructed not to tear off more shingles than they can replace in one day, so that your home is not exposed to inclement weather. If you need to reschedule your build, you must give us 24 hours notice.
13. Nails that fall from the roof during the job will be picked up with a magnetic nail roller. We will make every effort to pick up all nails. Please be advised, however, that some nails (hidden in the grass or shrubbery) may remain. Please make note and take precautions such as wearing shoes while outside. Please notify us immediately if the clean-up is unsatisfactory and we will send a representative to clean the property to your satisfaction.
4220 Proton Road, Suite 150, Dallas, TX 75244
Office: 833-799-ROOF
www.tcroofingexperts.com
8
HOW TO PREPARE
AND WHAT TO EXPECT
14. Occasionally, shingles have been sealed to the side of your exterior walls/siding. Age along with moisture saturation can rot the wood, stucco, or fiber cement board. During the removal of shingles along walls, paint removal and chipping of the siding may occur due to the existing rot. TC Roofing & Restorations is not responsible for this unavoidable occurrence.
15. When we remove layers of shingles from your roof, your roof's line may drop. This will be evident along any wall. The change in appearance will be minimal and will not affect the integrity and utility of the roof and flashing.
16. When the old roofing is removed, a tremendous amount of weight is taken off the frame of your house, causing slight movements of your rafters and beams. The shifting may cause small stress cracks in your vaulted ceiling along with paint popping off the nail heads. TC Roofing & Restorations is not responsible for this unavoidable occurrence.
17. Sagging or bubbling on the decking, called roof deflections, will not be corrected with standard re-roofing. This is an issue caused by improper framing when the home was framed. TC Roofing & Restorations is not responsible for these deflections unless covered by your insurance. We are not re-framing the home, we are re-roofing the home.
18. TC Roofing & Restorations will replace and cure any items damaged during the construction process, but only items directly damaged during the construction process. TC Roofing & Restorations will repair or replace what is damaged, but it is not responsible for matching of these items. Please make sure to walk around with your project manager before your build to identify existing damage on the property.
19. If there are AC lines or any other objects attached to the underside of the decking that a nail could penetrate or damage, they must be removed or reset to avoid damage from nailing shingles. We will inspect and notify you before the project of the presence of these lines so the Property Owner can have a licensed HVAC or Electrical Company to detach and reset these lines. Please ask your Project Manager if they have inspected the attic for this potential issue.
20. Certain homes may be equipped with gas flue vents which may become detached or knocked loose. It is recommended that we coordinate to inspect these vents before and after the project to remedy anything that may have been altered during the build. Please inform TC Roofing & Restorations about the presence and locations of these vents in your home. We must have access to the attic immediately after the project is complete. If we cannot have access into the home, we can not be responsible for any damages. PLEASE COORDINATE with your Project Manager to inspect the attic IMMEDIATELY after the build is complete.
21. For homes with no drip edge flashing or homes with no pest control warranty: TC Roofing & Restorations will be installing a drip edge flashing per Georgia building code. This flashing will seal the gap between your fascia board and decking. If you have pests (squirrels or rodents) in your attic currently. This flashing will seal them in the attic and they will try to find a way out. If you hear rodent activity after your roof is installed, you should call a pest control company. TC Roofing & Restorations will not be held responsible for the cost of rodent removal after your new roof is installed.
22. If any damages are established to be the fault of TC Roofing & Restorations, the homeowner shall notify TC Roofing & Restorations within 2 weeks of the completion of the project, so that we are given an opportunity to cure the issue. TC Roofing & Restorations will not compensate for the damage, but will correct all workmanship issues. The 2 weeks is not the warranty, but the time frame to raise any visual or technical issues with roof construction.
23. Fees related to waste disposal are typically covered by insurance, calculated based on the predicted quantity and nature of the material. Any additional waste isn't factored into the budget, nor is there space for miscellaneous items. Any alterations or negotiations must be directly communicated with and paid to the crew.
4220 Proton Road, Suite 150, Dallas, TX 75244
Office: 833-799-ROOF
www.tcroofingexperts.com

I understand that this authorization will remain in effect until the debt is fully discharged or I cancel it in writing which ever comes first, and I agree to notify the business in writing of any changes in my account information or termination of this authorization at least 15 days prior to the next billing date. If the above noted payment date falls on a weekend or holiday, I understand that the payment may be executed on the next business day. For ACH debits to my checking/savings account, I understand that because this is an electronic transaction, these funds may be withdrawn from my account as soon as the above noted periodic transaction dates. In the case of an ACH Transaction being rejected for Non Sufficient Funds (NSF) I understand that TC Roofing & Restorations may at its discretion attempt to process the charge again within 30 days, and agree to an additional $5.00 charge for each attempt returned NSF, which will be initiated as a separate transaction from the authorized recurring payment. I acknowledge that the origination of ACH transactions to my account must comply with the provisions of U.S. law. I certify that I am an authorized user of this credit card/bank account and will not dispute TC Roofing & Restorations billing with my bank or credit card company; so long as the transaction corresponds to the terms indicated in this agreement.

🔨 Trade Notes

Keep all notes specific to each trade. DO NOT CROSS TRADES or the app will send a demon to your house to play really loud reggae music at 3am every night.
